title: 12   Laboratory Manual 4. Represent A 3   as in Fig. 1.4
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - the other end to move freely along the circle
activityTitle: 12   Laboratory Manual 4. Represent A 3   as in Fig. 1.4
objective:
To verify that for two sets A and B,
n   (A×B) =   pq   and the total number of
relations from A to B is 2 pq , where
n (A) =   p   and   n (B) =   q .
Paper, different coloured pencils.
procedure:
1. Take a set A 1   which has one element (say)   a 1 , and take another set B 1 , which
has one element (say)   b 1 .
2. Take a set A 2   which has two elements (say)   a 1   and   a 2   and take another set B 3 ,
which has three elements (say)   b 1   ,   b 2   and   b 3 .
3. Take a set A 3   which has three elements (say)   a 1 ,   a 2   and   a 3 , and take another
set B 4 , which has four elements (say)   b 1   ,   b 2   ,   b 3   and   b 4 .
observation:
1. Represent all the possible correspondences of the elements of set A 1   to the
elements of set B 1   visually as shown in Fig. 2.1.
discussion: The activity can be used for calculating the number of subsets of a given set.
